title: Support for sidecars in `kube play`
body:
### Feature request description

In k8s, initContainers with `restart: Always` continue to run in the pod as sidecars (which is useful when the order of startup and shutdown matters between components that also continue to run concurrently)

I have some use-cases where podman is a better choice of "orchestration" instead of a single node cluster but the workloads include sidecars

### Suggest potential solution

initContainers with `restart: Always` should continue to run while the next container should be started once the health probes show it running.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The other options are 
- Modifying the yaml to use normal containers and adding logic to the container images to delay startup
- Don't use podman kube play and manually rewrite the yaml into a quadlet
- Use a single node k3s cluster

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Yes, currently Podman disregards the restart policy set at the init container. However, this ability in Podman predates the same in K8S. So, the way it is supported is by setting the annotation: `io.podman.annotations.init.container.type` to `always`
(See MAN page: https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.2.1/markdown/podman-kube-play.1.html). Having said that, if I read the code correctly, the annotation is at the pod level and as a result will affect all init containers.

From what I can tell, a fix that will allow the required granularity should be around here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L939

> if I read the code correctly, the annotation is at the pod level and as a result will affect all init containers.

This was my experience and it means that you can't mix types of initContainers :( 

Thank you for the pointer to the right place in the code. I'll try to get some time free to attempt a PR

Thanks for helping on this. There might be one more thing to consider.
The definition of probes should also be allowed. In [Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/sidecar-containers/#sidecar-containers-and-pod-lifecycle), a sidecar container relies on a `readinessProbe` to signal that the next init container can start. 
If that annotation is `always`, defining probes still triggers the same error. I haven't tested the case without probes, but I guess the next init container just runs right after successful startup of the current one.
